title: MATRIX Core Event Schema v0.2
description: Core schema for MATRIX verifiable AI governance event records. Defines the minimal traceable, auditable, and responsibility-preserving event structure for institutional, procurement, and cyber-incident domains. Privacy-by-design: only references, identifiers, URIs, or cryptographic hashes are permitted in input/output fields; raw personal data MUST NOT be embedded.

schema_version.description: Constant identifier of the MATRIX schema version used to produce this event record.

event_id.description: Unique event identifier. UUIDv7 is recommended to ensure time-ordered replay consistency and deterministic event-chain reconstruction across distributed audit logs.

event_timestamp.description: RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 timestamp in UTC marking the moment the event was generated.

domain_extension.description: Governance domain to which this event belongs. Determines applicable extension profile and audit obligations.

canonicalization_method.description: Canonicalization method applied to the event payload before hashing. JCS / RFC 8785 ensures deterministic JSON serialization for reproducible hash verification.

hash_algorithm.description: Cryptographic hash algorithm used to compute previous_event_hash and trace_hash.

previous_event_hash.description: Hash of the previous event in the chain, computed using the declared canonicalization_method and hash_algorithm. Enables tamper-evident event chaining. For genesis events, an all-zero digest of the algorithm length MUST be used.

trace_hash.description: Hash of the canonicalized current event payload, excluding the trace_hash field itself, computed using the declared canonicalization_method and hash_algorithm. Anchors the event in the verifiable trace.

AI_system_used.description: Identification of the AI system or model involved in generating, supporting, or processing the event. Decision-making authority remains with the human responsibility_owner.

system_id.description: Stable identifier of the AI system, model, or service.

version.description: Version, build, or model revision used at event time.

provider.description: Organisation or vendor responsible for the AI system.

role.description: Functional role of the AI system in this event. Public authority decisions MUST NOT be fully automated.

input_reference.description: Reference, identifier, URI, or cryptographic hash pointing to the input artefact. Privacy-by-design: MUST NOT contain raw personal, sensitive, or otherwise identifying data.

output_reference.description: Reference, identifier, URI, or cryptographic hash pointing to the output artefact. Privacy-by-design: MUST NOT contain raw personal, sensitive, or otherwise identifying data.

human_validation.description: Record of mandatory human oversight applied to this event. Preserves human-in-the-loop accountability.

validated.description: Indicates whether a competent human validated the event or its underlying decision.

validator_id.description: Pseudonymous or institutional identifier of the validating human. MUST NOT contain raw personal data.

validation_timestamp.description: Timestamp of the human validation act.

validation_notes_reference.description: Reference, URI, or hash pointing to validation notes or rationale. MUST NOT embed raw content.

retention_requirement.description: Data retention policy applicable to this event record.

retention_period_days.description: Mandatory retention period in days, derived from applicable legal or institutional obligation.

legal_basis.description: Legal or regulatory basis grounding the retention requirement.

disposal_action.description: Action to be taken at the end of the retention period.

drift_indicator.description: Indicator of model, behavioural, or distributional drift relevant to the AI system used.

drift_detected.description: Whether drift was detected at event time.

detection_method_reference.description: Reference, URI, or hash pointing to the drift detection method or report.

fail_closed.description: Fail-closed governance state. When integrity, validation, or oversight conditions are not met, the system MUST default to a non-permissive closed state.

enabled.description: Whether fail-closed governance is enforced for this event.

triggered.description: Whether the fail-closed state was triggered during this event.

reason.description: Reason or condition that triggered the fail-closed state, if applicable.

audit_status.description: Current audit status of the event record.

escalation_status.description: Escalation lifecycle state for incidents, anomalies, or contested events.

responsibility_owner.description: Human or institutional entity bearing legal and operational responsibility for this event. Responsibility MUST remain attributable to a human or accountable institution.

owner_id.description: Pseudonymous or institutional identifier of the responsible party.

owner_role.description: Institutional role or function of the responsibility owner.

organisation.description: Public authority, agency, or accountable institution.
